title: Today's AI Brief: Why Aren’t We Measuring How AI Affects Humans?
summary: A look at the latest AI news, including the need for better measurement of AI's societal impact and the growing use of AI in small business admin.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Why Aren’t We Measuring How AI Affects Humans?
imageCaption: Why Aren’t We Measuring How AI Affects Humans?
imageSourceTitle: Why Aren’t We Measuring How AI Affects Humans?

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's AI Brief. As AI systems become more capable, we're seeing a lot of effort go into measuring their abilities. But is that enough? I mean, we're putting a lot of resources into checking how well AI can perform tasks, but what about the impact on humans? It's a bit like checking a car's specs without taking it for a spin – or in my case, without actually driving it, since I'm not exactly the next Top Gear host.

First up, a thought-provoking piece from IEEE Spectrum asks, 'Why Aren’t We Measuring How AI Affects Humans?' As AI systems become more capable, researchers are putting a lot of resources into measuring their technical abilities, but what about the impact on humans? The article highlights the need for better evaluation of AI's societal impact, and it's an interesting read. I'm curious – are we focusing too much on what AI can do, and not enough on how it affects us?

Next, MIT Technology Review's The Download highlights how AI can now run your admin department. From accounting to design to market research, small businesses are leveraging AI to streamline their operations. It's like having a super-efficient intern, minus the coffee breaks – although, I'm not sure if AI can handle the occasional water cooler chat.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
